features.name: Commit Analyze Decode Fix
features.description: Commit the working-tree fix for the Analyze 'data couldn't be read' decode error: mo analyze omits is_dir for large_files entries, so DiskEntry.isDir was made optional with a ?? false fallback in the view.
doneCriteria: Sources/Models/SystemStatus.swift DiskEntry.isDir is Bool? (optional)
Sources/Views/AnalyzeView.swift uses (entry.isDir ?? false)
make app; Analyze tab renders the large-files list with no decode error
Committed as a single commit

subTasks.name: Runtime-verify Analyze tab decodes + renders
subTasks.evidence: Root cause confirmed against real mo 1.40.0 output: analyze --json entries[] carry is_dir but large_files[] omit it (sample keys ['name','path','size']), so non-optional isDir failed to decode the large-files list. Optional fix decodes both.

features.evidence: DiskEntry.isDir made Bool? (SystemStatus.swift); AnalyzeView uses (entry.isDir ?? false). Root cause verified: large_files[] omit is_dir while entries[] include it. swift build passes. Committed on branch feat/drilbur-and-next-tasks.

features.name: Parser-Resilience Regression Harness
features.description: Guard the fragile human-readable-text parsers and literal-string matches that silently return empty results if Mole's output format changes: parseCleanPreview, parsePurgePreview, parseOptimizePreview, and the literal 'System-level cleanup skipped, requires sudo' match. Capture real mo output as golden fixtures and add a parser test that fails on format drift.
doneCriteria: Golden-output fixtures captured from real mo (clean preview clean-list.txt, purge, optimize) on Mole 1.40.0
A parser test feeds the fixtures through the parsers and asserts the expected non-empty structured results
Test asserts the 'System-level cleanup skipped, requires sudo' detection still matches
Mutating a glyph marker (===, the box rule, the arrow) or the skip string in a fixture makes the test fail
Test runs via make test (or a documented command if a test target must be added)

subTasks.name: Capture golden fixtures from mo 1.40.0
subTasks.evidence: Tests/DrilburTests/Fixtures: clean-list.txt (REAL, 15 === sections / 46 # size lines from mo clean --dry-run), optimize.txt (REAL, 22 ➤ steps from mo optimize --dry-run). purge.txt is format-verified (━━━ confirmed at Vendor/Mole/bin/purge.sh:40; size-line convention shared with clean) because this machine had no purgeable artifacts — provenance documented in Fixtures/README.md.

subTasks.name: Add parser test against fixtures
subTasks.evidence: Extracted pure parsers into Sources/Services/MoOutputParser.swift (parseCleanList, parsePurgeText, parseByteSize, purgeCategoryToType, systemCleanupSkipMarker/detectsSystemCleanupSkip); MoService + CleanView delegate. New DrilburTests XCTest target (Package.swift), 8 tests, pass via `make parser-test`. Coverage honestly: clean (REAL fixture) and optimize/StepStreamParser (REAL fixture, 22 steps) are genuine golden guards against parser-code regressions. The purge assertion is weaker — its fixture is synthetic and matches the parser's expected shape (somewhat circular; purge's real stdout format is unconfirmed). testSystemCleanupSkipMarkerLiteral is near-tautological (constant == its own literal): it guards against accidental edits to the constant, NOT against clean.sh changing its wording.

subTasks.name: Verify drift (mutated fixture) fails the test
subTasks.evidence: Two in-suite drift tests (=== -> ###, ━━━ -> ===) assert empty results. Also proven empirically: sed-mutating clean-list.txt === markers made testCleanListParsesRealFixture FAIL (got 0 categories, all 3 asserts fired); restoring the fixture made it pass.

features.evidence: XCTest target chosen (user decision). Pure parsers extracted to MoOutputParser.swift; MoService/CleanView delegate. 8 tests pass (`make parser-test`); make build + make app unaffected. SCOPE (honest): this is a static golden-fixture suite — it guards the parser CODE against regressions and pins the expected format, and a live fixture mutation was shown to fail the test. It does NOT auto-detect live Mole format drift; fixtures are snapshots that must be re-captured on a Vendor/Mole bump (documented in Fixtures/README.md + CLAUDE.md). Clean/optimize fixtures are real; purge fixture is synthetic; the skip-marker test is near-tautological. Runtime-verified the refactored delegation on REAL output: a live `mo clean --dry-run` in the bundled app rendered 5 categories / 3.51GB via MoService.parseCleanPreview -> MoOutputParser.parseCleanList. CLAUDE.md + Makefile updated (the 'no test target' principle is now scoped to one parser-only target; UI/runtime still verified by build+launch).

features.name: Resolve Deferred FDA Not-Granted Check

doneCriteria: With FDA revoked for the app and relaunched, Permissions card + preflight banners read 'Not granted'
If still 'Granted', PermissionsService.probeFullDiskAccess repointed from ~/Library/.../TCC.db to /Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db and re-verified
feat-008 FDA subTask flipped from manual-check to done with evidence
session-handoff.md manual-check note resolved

subTasks.name: Revoke FDA, relaunch, observe Permissions card + banners
subTasks.evidence: The rename gave Drilbur a new bundle id (co.greenpassport.drilbur) that has never been granted FDA — so 'not granted' is its default, no manual revoke needed. Launched via `open --env DRILBUR_SCREEN=permissions /Applications/Drilbur.app` (own responsible process): Permissions card reads 'Not granted' (amber). screenshot /tmp/drilbur-perm-open.png.

subTasks.name: Repoint probe path if false-positive
subTasks.evidence: Not needed — probeFullDiskAccess already reads the system path /Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db (repointed from ~/Library in commit c4fd444). The 'Granted' seen when direct-exec'ing the binary from an FDA-enabled terminal is macOS TCC responsible-process inheritance (child attributed to the terminal), NOT a probe bug: launching via LaunchServices (open) correctly reports 'Not granted'.

subTasks.name: Update feat-008 subTask + handoff
subTasks.evidence: feat-008 FDA subTask flipped manual-check -> done; session-handoff.md note resolved.

features.evidence: FDA not-granted branch verified (no code change needed). Probe path already correct (system /Library TCC.db since c4fd444). Root-caused the earlier 'Granted' false-positive to terminal-launch FDA inheritance, not the probe: a LaunchServices-launched Drilbur (new bundle id, no FDA) correctly shows 'Not granted' on the Permissions card. Bonus: Analyze tab loaded a 161GB analysis with no decode error, confirming feat-010 in the bundled app.

features.name: Consistent Error-State UX

subTasks.name: Build reusable error-state component
subTasks.evidence: Sources/Views/Components/ErrorStateView.swift: triangle icon + 'Something went wrong' title + message + optional Retry button, all DesignTokens (Color.danger/primary/secondary, Font.section/caption, .bordered button). No hardcoded colors.

subTasks.name: Wire into mo-backed tabs
subTasks.evidence: StatusView, CleanView, AnalyzeView, PurgeView, UninstallView now render ErrorStateView with their load fn as retry (refresh/loadPreview/loadAnalysis/loadProjects/loadApps); 5 duplicate per-view errorView helpers removed. OptimizeView keeps its contextual streaming banner by design (different interaction).

subTasks.name: Force-failure runtime verify + screenshot
subTasks.evidence: Forced failure via chmod -x bundled mo + DRILBUR_MO_PATH=stub (--version ok, status exit 1, so the runtime-available guard passes and the per-tab load fails). Screenshot: Status tab shows the new ErrorStateView (triangle, 'Something went wrong', message, Retry). Mascot deferred (no image asset; would be invented). Recovery verified by construction: Retry calls the same refresh()/load that the normal launch screenshot shows producing a working dashboard. Bundled mo exec bit restored.

features.evidence: Reusable ErrorStateView shared across the 5 mo-backed tabs; removed 5 near-duplicate errorView helpers. make build + make app + make parser-test (8/8) pass. Runtime-verified via screenshots: error state renders with Retry on failure; normal Status dashboard renders when mo works (error state is correctly conditional, no regression). DesignTokens only.

subTasks.name: Icon pipeline
subTasks.evidence: scripts/make_icon.sh: Swift/CoreGraphics compositor draws the art on a squircle (0.2237 corner ratio, ~9% margin, white->cool-grey gradient bg that blends the art's white bg, hairline edge) -> 1024 master -> sips iconset -> iconutil. AppIcon.icns now multi-res (ic12, 1MB) vs the prior single 128px. 475px source upscaled to 1024 (slightly soft, accepted). icon.png + SidebarLogo.png (256) emitted.

subTasks.name: Accent recolor

features.evidence: Drilbur identity applied: new multi-res app icon from the provided art, app-wide navy/indigo accent, Drilbur sidebar brand mark. Copyrighted art committed per maintainer's explicit choice (personal use).

features.name: In-App Uninstaller (multi-select, leftover preview, sortable list)
features.description: Turn the read-only Uninstall browser into a working uninstaller. Multi-select apps, preview each one's bundle + leftover files via a dry-run, then move them to the Trash, all without dropping to Terminal. Admin-required apps (root-owned bundles, Homebrew casks) are detected and deferred (non-selectable, badged), kept in the same list rather than a separate section. Add Finder-style column sorting by name and size.
doneCriteria: Select one or more user-owned apps and uninstall them with their leftovers in-app (no Terminal)
A dry-run preview shows each app's bundle + leftover paths and the true total before anything is deleted (preview-before-delete guard armed only on a non-empty parse)
Removal routes to the Trash (recoverable) and resolves ~/Library leftovers in the user's context
Admin-required apps are detected (root-owned / non-writable parent / Homebrew) and non-selectable, so an admin app can never poison an unprivileged batch
The execute re-validates the live selection against the previewed set
Sortable columns: name and size, ascending and descending
make build + make parser-test pass; real uninstall verified landing in Trash

subTasks.name: Model + parser
subTasks.evidence: AppInfo gains source/path/requiresAdmin; UninstallPreview/UninstallPreviewApp added. MoOutputParser.parseUninstallPreview parses the 'Files to be removed:' / glyph block with ANSI stripping; golden fixture uninstall-preview.txt + 5 tests (incl. drift, spaces, missing-terminator).

subTasks.name: Service
subTasks.evidence: MoService.streamFeeding(args:input:) feeds y\n (uninstall-only; stream/streamElevated keep nullDevice). bundleRequiresAdmin mirrors Mole's needs_sudo. finalizeUninstallPreview arms uninstallPreviewReady only on non-empty parse. getUninstallList computes requiresAdmin.

subTasks.name: View + sort
subTasks.evidence: UninstallView: multi-select rows (admin rows non-selectable + Admin badge), select-all, confirmation sheet listing leftovers, streamed execute, selection re-validated at execute, Finder-style sortable columns (default size-descending).

subTasks.name: Adversarial review + fixes
subTasks.evidence: 25-agent review; 12 confirmed findings triaged. Fixed: preview/execute set re-validation, live admin re-check, isPreviewing/sheet ordering, banner-flicker fold, missing-terminator test, README locale note. Refuted by primary source: 'admin detection misses system leftovers' (Mole forces system_files='' before its needs_sudo check).

features.evidence: Shipped on main (commit 4ac5645). make build + make parser-test (17 cases) pass. Real uninstall of a disposable bundle removed app + leftover, landed in ~/.Trash, exit 0, no hang. Admin detection verified against real stat (user-owned selectable, root-owned flagged). App launches to the Uninstall screen and renders (checkboxes, Admin badges, size-descending sort). Admin-app uninstall and an admin pre-authorize button were both discussed and deliberately deferred.
